description: End-to-end documentation, testing, and notebook compatibility for the adm CLI

userStories.title: Document adm document command in README
userStories.description: As a user reading the README, I want to see the document command listed with usage examples so I know how to generate a repository review.
acceptanceCriteria: adm document section added to Commands in README matching existing style
Mentions smart filtering: respects .gitignore, skips low-signal artifacts (mlruns/, logs, binary files)
Shows example for local path, remote Git URL, and default (current directory)
Mentions endpoint auto-selection behavior in one sentence
Lists key options: --source, --output, --endpoint, --max-file-chars, --max-total-chars
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document adm trigger command in README
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see adm trigger documented so I know it exists as a lightweight alternative to adm run.
acceptanceCriteria: adm trigger section added to Commands in README
Clearly states it re-runs an already-deployed job via SDK (no CLI needed, no redeploy)
Mentions notebook-native usage: from as_databricks_mlops import run_training_job
Shows the --target option
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document programmatic API for notebooks in README
userStories.description: As a user working inside a Databricks notebook, I want the README to show me how to pip-install the package and call its functions directly.
acceptanceCriteria: New Notebook Usage section added to README
Shows pip install command from GitHub
Shows from as_databricks_mlops import run_training_job example with argument
Shows from as_databricks_mlops import review_repository example for document generation
Notes which commands work in notebooks (trigger, document) vs which require local CLI (init, run)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Full README audit for staleness
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the README to be accurate against the current CLI surface.
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 commands listed in Commands section
Package version / naming references are accurate (as-databricks-mlops, adm)
No dead references to removed features or wrong flag names
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add --no-validate flag to adm init
userStories.description: As a notebook user, I want adm init to skip databricks bundle validate so I can scaffold projects in environments without the CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: --no-validate flag added to adm init click command
When passed, prints 'Skipping bundle validation (--no-validate).' and skips _run_bundle_validate
Test in test_cli.py verifies the flag works and output message appears
adm init --help shows --no-validate with description
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add automated tests for binary file exclusion in snapshots
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pytest coverage proving binary files (.pkl, .parquet, .pt, .npy) are excluded from repository snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates tmp_path with .pkl, .parquet, .pt, .npy files alongside a .py file
Asserts only the .py file appears in snapshot.files
Asserts binary files appear in snapshot.omitted_files with correct reason
Test is in tests/test_cli.py
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in f224afd as test_collect_repository_snapshot_excludes_binary_files. Review only.

userStories.title: Add automated test for max-file-chars truncation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pytest coverage proving the --max-file-chars limit truncates large files correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a small file and a large file exceeding max_file_chars
Calls collect_repository_snapshot with a low max_file_chars value
Asserts the large file is included but truncated (truncated=True, characters == max_file_chars)
Asserts the small file is not truncated
Asserts omitted_files contains a truncation reason for the large file
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in f224afd as test_collect_repository_snapshot_truncates_large_files. Review only.

userStories.title: Add automated test for total prompt budget enforcement
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pytest coverage proving the --max-total-chars budget stops including files once the limit is reached.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates 3 files of 100 chars each, calls snapshot with max_total_chars=150
Asserts total_characters <= 150
Asserts at least one file is in omitted_files with a budget-related reason
Test is in tests/test_cli.py
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed in f224afd as test_collect_repository_snapshot_respects_total_budget. Review only.

userStories.title: Build Databricks E2E test notebook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an automated test notebook that pip-installs the package inside a Databricks notebook and validates review_repository and run_training_job.
acceptanceCriteria: Notebook at tests/notebook_e2e_test.py with Databricks notebook source header
Test 1: package import and version check
Test 2: snapshot collection without LLM call (binary exclusion verified)
Test 3: clone a remote repo and collect snapshot
Test 4: full review_repository() call with real LLM endpoint
Test 5: run_training_job() with widget-provided job name
Notebook excluded from local pytest via tests/conftest.py collect_ignore
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: E2E validation: adm document on local messy-ml-project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that adm document generates a valid review document when pointed at the bundled example project.
acceptanceCriteria: adm document --source examples/messy-ml-project exits 0
Output file contains HTML comment header (source, endpoint, timestamp)
Output file contains Markdown review with headings (355+ lines)
.adm_internal/latest-review-prompt.txt was written
21 files included, 9 omitted (binary/low-signal artifacts excluded)
userStories.notes: Validated manually. Endpoint: databricks-gpt-5-4-mini. Review only.

userStories.title: E2E validation: adm document on remote Git URL
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that adm document can clone and review a remote repository.
acceptanceCriteria: adm document --source https://github.com/homayoon-moradi_data/az-databricks-mlops.git exits 0
Output file contains valid Markdown review (384+ lines)
51 files included from cloned repo
Temp clone directory cleaned up after completion
userStories.notes: Validated manually. Endpoint: databricks-gpt-5-4-mini. Review only.

userStories.title: E2E validation: full MLOps pipeline (init -> run -> job success)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the core MLOps flow works end-to-end in Databricks.
acceptanceCriteria: adm clean in examples/messy-ml-project succeeds (13 files removed)
adm init with flags succeeds, bundle validates
adm run deploys bundle and starts training job
Databricks job completes: Train SUCCESS, ModelValidation SUCCESS, ModelDeployment SUCCESS
Model registered in Unity Catalog with champion alias
userStories.notes: Validated manually. Job run 1122003268392490 completed all 3 tasks with SUCCESS. Review only.

userStories.title: Fix adm trigger job name to match DAB development mode prefix
userStories.description: As a user, I want adm trigger to find my deployed job even when DAB prepends [dev username] to the job name in development mode.
acceptanceCriteria: In trigger.py, change job matching from exact name match to suffix/contains match that handles the DAB [target username] prefix
adm trigger --target dev finds job named '[dev homayoon_moradi] dev-project-model-training-job'
Still raises ValueError when no job matches at all
Still raises ValueError when multiple jobs match
Add test in test_cli.py for trigger job name matching with DAB prefix
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Fixed: suffix match in trigger.py. 4 tests added. Verified live against deployed job.

userStories.title: Add --no-validate to adm new if it calls bundle validate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want adm new to also support --no-validate so notebook users can scaffold new projects without the databricks CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: Check if adm new also calls _run_bundle_validate; if so, add --no-validate flag matching the adm init implementation
Add test in test_cli.py verifying --no-validate skips validation for adm new
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: adm new does NOT call _run_bundle_validate — only prints a hint. No flag needed.

userStories.title: Update PRD Non-Goals to reflect --no-validate decision
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the PRD to accurately reflect that --no-validate was added.
acceptanceCriteria: In tasks/prd-adm-e2e-docs-and-testing.md, update Non-Goals to remove 'No new CLI flags or commands'
Add a note that --no-validate was added per Decision #2
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated Non-Goals with strikethrough and Decision #2 reference.

userStories.title: Update notebook E2E test to use correct DAB job name format
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Databricks test notebook to use the correct job name format so the run_training_job test passes.
acceptanceCriteria: In tests/notebook_e2e_test.py, update the job name widget hint to mention the [dev username] prefix pattern
Add a comment explaining the DAB development mode naming convention
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated widget hint to explain DAB prefix and suffix matching.
